methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $2.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Aetherflux, a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $60M+, is valued here against a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage companies in the same sector, including Aalyria, Stoke Space, Hubble Network, and Sift.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using a $2.0B base derived from total capital raised and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a $3.8B valuation. Peer-median methodology applied the central valuation of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Aalyria ($1.3B), Stoke Space ($1.0B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—yielding a $700M implied value. The range across these four peers spans $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied given the absence of Aetherflux revenue data, though the public Space & Aerospace sector median P/S multiple stands at 45.3x across 6 tickers.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $595M (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median of $2.2B. The wide spread reflects material tension between stage-progression mechanics and current peer valuations. Key uncertainties include the robustness of the $2.0B raised proxy, the representativeness of the four private comps given sector heterogeneity, and undisclosed revenue trajectory for public-comp calibration.
